bins.name: Expedited studies with dispatch control
bins.desc: SMECO supports expedited interconnection studies for large loads that agree to be dispatchable, provided that PJM retains adequate ability to control such facilities through curtailment and dispatch mechanisms such as Price Responsive Demand and Demand Response products.
quotes: SMECO urges the Commission, in any final rule, to ensure that PJM's ability to control such eligible load facilities through curtailment and/or dispatch, through, for example PJM Price Responsive Demand and Demand response products, to the extent those or similar products is permitted at all to serve new large loads, is adequate for PJM to integrate the facility into both operations and system planning.

bins.name: Large loads bear upgrade costs
bins.desc: SMECO supports requiring large loads and hybrid facilities to bear 100% of network upgrade costs assigned through interconnection studies via a federal framework analogous to Contribution in Aid of Construction.
quotes: SMECO supports a federal construct and framework in the PJM Open Access Transmission Tariff that would directly assign costs associated with upgrades to the electric transmission system caused by a large load facility directly to the large load facility (or group of large load facilities) assigned through the interconnection studies.

A framework that would directly assign costs associated with upgrades to the electric transmission system caused by a large load facility - akin to a customer Contribution in Aid of Construction ("CIAC") at the wholesale level or Federal CIAC - would also entail the new large load paying network service thru the applicable network service rate once access to the grid is achieved by paying for the appurtenant necessary upgrade.

bins.name: Standardized orderly interconnection procedures
bins.desc: SMECO calls for a final rule establishing standardized, non-discriminatory procedures for orderly interconnections of large new loads without compromising reliability.
quotes: SMECO respectfully submits that a final rule to establish standardized, non-discriminatory procedures for "orderly" interconnections of large new loads without compromising reliability is needed at the present time.

bins.name: Cost causation for residential rate protection
bins.desc: SMECO supports cost-causation ratemaking principles that directly assign transmission upgrade costs to large loads to protect residential customers from cost shifting and rate increases, as PJM transmission costs already exceed capacity costs and will rise further.
quotes: SMECO supports a federal construct and framework in the PJM Open Access Transmission Tariff that would directly assign costs associated with upgrades to the electric transmission system caused by a large load facility directly to the large load facility (or group of large load facilities) assigned through the interconnection studies.

Principle 8 is essential to keep rates affordable to other consumers (especially residential customers, many of whom are currently struggling to afford their electric service) and to protect them from cost shifting.

PJM transmission costs already occupy a higher share of total wholesale costs than RPM capacity costs, and such costs stand to increase as the considerable large loads contemplated for the PJM region require PJM grid expansion.

SMECO's support for a framework in the PJM Tariff that would directly assign costs associated with upgrades to the electric transmission system caused by a large load facility directly to the large load facility is consistent with long-standing cost-causation ratemaking principles

bins.name: RMR studies for co-located hybrid arrangements
bins.desc: SMECO supports expanding reliability must-run studies for existing generators entering partial suspension to serve co-located or hybrid large load configurations to ensure local reliability is not compromised.
quotes: SMECO appreciates the importance of ANOPR Principle 10 and urges the Commission to adopt a final rule to ensure that local reliability is not negatively impacted by hybrid/co-location arrangements, if such arrangements are to be permitted in the first instance at all.

SMECO supports a final rule that would consider expanding the reliability studies that PJM performs pursuant to PJM Tariff, Part V to an existing generating facility that seeks to enter a partial suspension, as described in the ANOPR, to serve any new load authorized to take service in a co-located or hybrid configuration.

bins.name: Operational and planning integration capability
bins.desc: SMECO emphasizes that control mechanisms for dispatchable large loads must be adequate for PJM to integrate such facilities into both operations and system planning.
quotes: SMECO respectfully submits that a final rule to establish standardized, non-discriminatory procedures for "orderly" interconnections of large new loads without compromising reliability is needed at the present time.

SMECO urges the Commission, in any final rule, to ensure that PJM's ability to control such eligible load facilities through curtailment and/or dispatch, through, for example PJM Price Responsive Demand and Demand response products, to the extent those or similar products is permitted at all to serve new large loads, is adequate for PJM to integrate the facility into both operations and system planning.

bins.name: Local supply adequacy in hybrid arrangements
bins.desc: SMECO warns that co-located hybrid large loads may dilute available generation supply and degrade resource adequacy, necessitating protective reliability frameworks.
quotes: SMECO appreciates the importance of ANOPR Principle 10 and urges the Commission to adopt a final rule to ensure that local reliability is not negatively impacted by hybrid/co-location arrangements, if such arrangements are to be permitted in the first instance at all.

bins.name: PJM-specific concerns and context
bins.desc: SMECO addresses large load growth in PJM auctions and identifies PJM as the primary region for its comments, noting rising transmission costs and the need for regional flexibility in implementing large load rules.
quotes: with each passing PJM capacity market auction, the quantity of new loads that PJM is attempting to serve in its capacity market is ever increasing.

PJM transmission costs already occupy a higher share of total wholesale costs than RPM capacity costs, and such costs stand to increase as the considerable large loads contemplated for the PJM region require PJM grid expansion.

bins.name: Rapid increase in large loads
bins.desc: SMECO documents that large load volume is rising with each capacity auction, creating urgency for interconnection frameworks.
quotes: with each passing PJM capacity market auction, the quantity of new loads that PJM is attempting to serve in its capacity market is ever increasing.

bins.name: Tariff products for dispatchable control
bins.desc: SMECO references PJM Price Responsive Demand and Demand Response products as mechanisms to enable adequate system control of dispatchable large loads.
quotes: SMECO urges the Commission, in any final rule, to ensure that PJM's ability to control such eligible load facilities through curtailment and/or dispatch, through, for example PJM Price Responsive Demand and Demand response products, to the extent those or similar products is permitted at all to serve new large loads, is adequate for PJM to integrate the facility into both operations and system planning.

bins.name: Cost-causation ratemaking principle
bins.desc: SMECO grounds its cost-allocation position in long-standing cost-causation principles requiring that costs be allocated commensurate with benefits derived.
quotes: SMECO supports a federal construct and framework in the PJM Open Access Transmission Tariff that would directly assign costs associated with upgrades to the electric transmission system caused by a large load facility directly to the large load facility (or group of large load facilities) assigned through the interconnection studies.

SMECO's support for a framework in the PJM Tariff that would directly assign costs associated with upgrades to the electric transmission system caused by a large load facility directly to the large load facility is consistent with long-standing cost-causation ratemaking principles

bins.name: CIAC framework for wholesale cost allocation
bins.desc: SMECO proposes a federal Contribution in Aid of Construction model at the wholesale level to directly assign upgrade costs to large loads and establish their obligation to pay network service rates.
quotes: A framework that would directly assign costs associated with upgrades to the electric transmission system caused by a large load facility - akin to a customer Contribution in Aid of Construction ("CIAC") at the wholesale level or Federal CIAC - would also entail the new large load paying network service thru the applicable network service rate once access to the grid is achieved by paying for the appurtenant necessary upgrade.

bins.name: Protecting residential customer interests
bins.desc: SMECO cites Maryland legislation stating that residential customers should not bear financial risk from large load interconnection, establishing a state policy underpinning federal cost allocation.
quotes: it is the intent of the General Assembly that residential retail electric customers in the State should not bear the financial risks associated with large load customers interconnecting to the electric system serving the State.

bins.name: Keeping rates affordable for existing customers
bins.desc: SMECO emphasizes that cost-causation cost allocation is essential to keep rates affordable for existing consumers, many of whom are struggling with affordability.
quotes: Principle 8 is essential to keep rates affordable to other consumers (especially residential customers, many of whom are currently struggling to afford their electric service) and to protect them from cost shifting.

bins.name: Growing PJM transmission cost burden
bins.desc: SMECO documents that transmission costs already occupy a higher share of PJM wholesale costs than capacity costs and will increase further due to grid expansion for large loads.
quotes: PJM transmission costs already occupy a higher share of total wholesale costs than RPM capacity costs, and such costs stand to increase as the considerable large loads contemplated for the PJM region require PJM grid expansion.

bins.name: Reliability risk in hybrid co-location
bins.desc: SMECO cautions that hybrid co-located arrangements could negatively impact local reliability through supply dilution and calls for protective frameworks.
quotes: SMECO appreciates the importance of ANOPR Principle 10 and urges the Commission to adopt a final rule to ensure that local reliability is not negatively impacted by hybrid/co-location arrangements, if such arrangements are to be permitted in the first instance at all.

bins.name: Expand RMR studies for hybrid configurations
bins.desc: SMECO supports expanding PJM Tariff Part V reliability must-run studies to cover cases where existing generators partially suspend service to co-locate with new large loads.
quotes: SMECO supports a final rule that would consider expanding the reliability studies that PJM performs pursuant to PJM Tariff, Part V to an existing generating facility that seeks to enter a partial suspension, as described in the ANOPR, to serve any new load authorized to take service in a co-located or hybrid configuration.
